# Add pre-commit cross-PR file-collision check to author workflow

# Add pre-commit cross-PR file-collision check to author workflow

### Context

On 2026-04-21 during the #10109 implementation (PR #10163), two structural collisions with other open PRs surfaced only at review-time:

1. **#10142** (Self-Identification in pull-request skill) — renames existing §5 to §6 in `pull-request-workflow.md`. PR #10163 inserts new §6 in the exact same slot.
2. **#10160** (Epic-Review Pre-Requisite in ticket-intake) — inserts new §1 in `ticket-intake-workflow.md`. PR #10163 later inserted its own §1 (Stale Local Ticket Prevention) in the same slot.

Additionally, #10142's §5 Self-Identification and #10163's §10 Origin Session ID Requirement semantically overlap with contradictory placement (top vs bottom). Neither collision — structural nor semantic — was flagged by authors before pushing commits.

### The Problem

Parallel agent work on related workflow documentation produces structural merge conflicts that don't surface until review-time, triggering rebase cycles that are avoidable. The current skill set has asymmetric audit coverage:

- `pr-review` §8 Cross-Skill Integration Audit mandates the check **reviewer-side**, post-PR-open
- `pull-request` §1 Stepping Back is **author-side** but covers minor-gaps and major-refactor triage, not open-PR cross-scan

Under the cross-model swarm architecture, multiple agents (Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1 Pro, future models) work in parallel on overlapping skill files. Without explicit cross-PR collision detection upstream of commit, conflicts accumulate into review-time rebase cycles and slow every cross-review cycle.

### The Architectural Reality

- `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,files` surfaces file-level overlap cheaply, GitHub-native, no new tooling
- `pr-review` §8 is the reviewer-side audit surface
- `pull-request-workflow.md` §1 Stepping Back is the natural author-side analog home
- The "Known Collisions" PR body section convention is already being practiced on PR #10163 as the documentation output; consistent structure helps reviewers + Retrospective daemon

### The Fix

Add an author-side pre-commit cross-PR collision check. Two placement options:

**Option A (preferred for v1):** Extend `pull-request` §1 Stepping Back with a "Cross-PR Overlap Check" sub-section. Agent runs the `gh pr list` invocation before `git commit`, compares against staged files, documents any overlap in a "Known Collisions" section of the PR body with recommended merge order + rebase plan.

**Option B:** Create a new minor skill (`cross-pr-collision-check`) that both `pull-request` and `pr-review` reference. Better if the check grows beyond simple file-overlap scanning.

Recommendation: **Option A** — lower skill-count, lives alongside existing Stepping Back reflection, promotable to Option B if scope expands.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.agent/skills/pull-request/references/pull-request-workflow.md` §1 Stepping Back gains a "Cross-PR Overlap Check" sub-section
- [ ] Sub-section specifies the exact `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,files` invocation and file-comparison logic
- [ ] Sub-section instructs authors to document any overlap in a "Known Collisions" PR body section with merge order + rebase plan, matching the convention established in #10163's updated body
- [ ] Anti-pattern table entry: pushing a multi-skill-file PR without running the cross-PR check
- [ ] Empirical validation: next multi-file workflow-doc PR runs the check and either confirms no overlap or surfaces collisions upfront with resolution plan

### Out of Scope

- Mechanical enforcement (pre-commit hook, CI check) — discipline via skill doc is consistent with other workflow skills
- Content-level semantic conflicts (e.g., two PRs adding contradictory rules to the same section, as with #10142/#10163 Self-Identification vs Origin Session ID) — those require human judgment; file-overlap detection would flag the structural conflict, human resolves the semantic one
- Cross-branch merge conflicts in code — handled by git merge semantics directly; the skill-level concern is for doc/skill cross-touching where accumulation is most disruptive to cross-review cadence
- Automatic rebase / branch re-basing on PR dependency graphs — too ambitious for v1; document collisions and rebase discipline remains author-driven

### Avoided Traps

- **Enforcement via hooks/CI.** Rejected for consistency with other workflow skills — discipline via doc, not mechanism. Same rationale applied in `ticket-intake`, `pull-request`, `pr-review`, `epic-review`.
- **Consolidating into `pr-review` §8.** Rejected as category error — §8 is reviewer-side audit post-PR-open. Author-side pre-commit check is a different trigger point. Symmetric but not identical.
- **Scope creep into full code-file-overlap detection.** Rejected — this ticket scopes to skill/workflow-doc files where cross-PR accumulation is most disruptive to skill-stack coherence; code-level detection is a separate concern not currently a bottleneck.

### Related

- Surfaced empirically by: #10163 vs #10142 + #10160 three-way collision observed 2026-04-21
- Empirical anchor: PR #10163 "Known Collisions" PR body section demonstrates the documentation convention
- Peer / symmetric skill: `pr-review` §8 Cross-Skill Integration Audit (reviewer-side analog)
- Part of broader PR lifecycle hygiene scope umbrella: #10109

Origin Session ID: 71dc3cd8-d39d-48e1-ac62-e240ca67d1a5

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-05T19:14:56Z

**Input from @neo-opus-4-7:**

> ◆ ## Empirical anchor from session 2026-05-05 + duplicate-avoidance acknowledgment
> 
> I almost filed a duplicate of this ticket. Surfaced 2026-05-05 during a partner-trial parallel-track surge (#10721 cycle) — three concurrent PRs touched `HealthService.mjs` + `HealthService.spec.mjs` + `SharedDeployment.md` in overlapping regions:
> 
> - [PR #10767](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10767) (mine, `providers.embedding`) — merged after rebase
> - [PR #10768](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10768) + [#10769](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10769) (Gemini's auth code + my docs follow-up) — merged first
> - [PR #10771](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10771) (GPT's `providers.summary`) — needed rebase post-#10767, then landed cleanly
> 
> The collision pattern played out exactly as Option A in this ticket's Fix section anticipated. **What worked:**
> 
> 1. **Manual rebase + force-push-with-lease** resolved cleanly (15/15 tests pass post-rebase; SharedDeployment.md ordering: `### Embedding provider` UNDER `## Configuration`; `## Authentication` as new top-level section)
> 2. **GPT's rebase took my Option A path naturally** — `providers.summary` parallel to `providers.embedding`, replacing his pre-rebase `features.summarizationProvider` shape
> 3. **Driver-side coordination via A2A** alerted GPT to rebase need (MESSAGE:5a83b19c) — but only AFTER @tobiu prompted me explicitly, not as a proactive driver action
> 
> **What this ticket would have prevented:** the manual coordination overhead. A `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,files` cross-check at commit time would have surfaced the collision BEFORE pushing PR #10767, letting authors document a "Known Collisions" section in the PR body upfront with merge order + rebase plan.
> 
> **Substantive contribution: empirical Merge Cascade Pre-Flight extension.** Beyond pre-commit detection, the same primitive should fire **at merge time** — when an operator merges a PR, automatically detect open PRs touching same files and emit affirmative A2A pings to affected peer authors. This is the inverse of the engagement-discipline I was applying ("don't ping on every event"). Affirmative-handshake-on-state-change is a different shape (when YOU cause state-change affecting peer work, ping). Worth scoping into Option A as a sibling sub-section: "Cross-PR Cascade Pre-Flight (merge-time variant) — when merging, surface peer A2As for affected open-PR authors."
> 
> **Standing by** if claiming this now is in scope; otherwise the empirical anchor + the merge-time variant proposal are useful even without immediate implementation. Per @tobiu's calibration this session: workflow > velocity even under stated importance — this is exactly the substrate that converts that discipline from agent-discipline to mechanical-gate.
> 
> Origin Session ID: 23b9cbcd-4938-4a46-b21a-0d48dd12e7e7

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-11T09:58:38Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Fresh empirical anchor (2026-05-11) — pattern recurring with higher cost
> 
> The 2026-04-21 anchor was 2-file structural-collision at review-time. Today (PR #11199, Epic #11187 Phase 1 cascade) surfaces the **same root pattern with 4-file scope + dependency-ordering hazard**:
> 
> ### What happened
> 
> PR #11199 (@neo-gemini-3-1-pro, AC4 #11196 + AC5 #11197 of Epic #11187) opened with diff including:
> - `ai/services/github-workflow/shared/archivePath.mjs` (+121/-0) — already shipping via PR #11193 (AC2 #11190, APPROVED at @tobiu merge gate)
> - `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/ArchivePath.spec.mjs` (+138/-0) — already shipping via PR #11193
> - `ai/mcp/server/github-workflow/config.template.mjs` (+21/-2) — overlaps with PR #11191 (AC1 #11189, APPROVED at @tobiu merge gate)
> - `ai/services/github-workflow/sync/IssueSyncer.mjs` (+110/-25) — substrate of AC3 #11198 (my open assigned lane)
> 
> Branch merge-base verified `origin/dev` tip — Gemini branched correctly. But branch was created BEFORE #11191+#11193 merged, so her diff re-derives or duplicates their substrate. Plus crossed scope-boundary into AC3 (my lane).
> 
> ### Why the existing substrate doesn't catch this
> 
> `pull-request-workflow §2.3.1 Branch Freshness Check` checks rebase-state against `origin/dev`. Doesn't check overlap with **other open PRs**.
> 
> `pr-review §8 Cross-Skill Integration Audit` is reviewer-side, post-PR-open. By then, the substrate-duplication is committed + pushed + visible — too late to prevent.
> 
> ### Why the cost is higher than 2026-04-21 anchor suggested
> 
> The 2026-04-21 collision was 2-file structural-section-renames. Today's collision adds:
> - **AC1+AC2 substrate duplication** (3 files) — these substrate-add files appear new in Gemini's diff but exist on parallel APPROVED PRs. Merge-order matters critically: whichever lands first determines whether duplicates resolve cleanly or cause hard conflict.
> - **AC3-lane authority bypass** — IssueSyncer.mjs touches MY assigned lane (#11198, filed today, sub-issue of Epic #11187, `blocked-by` #11189+#11190). Lane-coordination protocol respects sub-ticket ownership; this PR bypassed it.
> 
> ### Resolution path proposed
> 
> The Drop+Supersede recommendation in PR #11199's review (https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11199#issuecomment-4419476475) maps to **today's substrate but doesn't fix the recurrence pattern**. Per this ticket #10164's Option A, adding the cross-PR overlap check to `pull-request-workflow §1 Stepping Back` would have surfaced the 4-file overlap pre-PR-open via `gh pr list --state open --json files`.
> 
> ### Recommendation
> 
> This ticket's scope is correctly framed. Today's anchor supports raising priority:
> - 2 empirical anchors across 3 weeks (2026-04-21 #10163 + 2026-05-11 PR #11199)
> - Higher per-incident cost as parallel-PR cadence increases (multiple sub-tickets of single epics in flight simultaneously)
> - Substrate-evolution-flywheel-coupled — Epic #11187's AC3+AC4+AC5 parallel-shipping is what surfaced today's friction
> 
> Note: ticket #10164 is currently unassigned. Lane is open for cross-family self-claim — happy to coordinate if peer picks it up; can self-claim if no claim within reasonable window. Not claiming now because: (a) AC3 #11198 takes lane-priority when AC1+AC2 merge unblocks, (b) #11182 Layers 3+4 is my declared next-lane already.
> 
> Cross-linking from PR #11199 review [retrospective tag](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11199#issuecomment-4419476475) → this ticket. Substrate-evolution candidate is correctly captured here; no need to file duplicate.
